pr.title: ft: decompile Kirby & Ice Climbers special moves
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled 17 fighter special-move functions across Kirby copy abilities and Ice Climbers special moves, added needed prototypes and Popo attribute fields, and reported 100% objdiff matches with main.dol SHA1 OK.

key_files.role: Decompiled Kirby Ness-copy neutral-B and Kirby Purin/Jigglypuff-copy neutral-B helper/collision functions; added ftparts include and static inline shared attribute reset helper.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftKb_SpecialNNs.c added 107 deleted 5
raw_diff: ftKb_SetNsFlashAttr, ftKb_NsSpecialNEnd_Anim, ftKb_NsSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNPr_80101560, ftKb_SpecialNPr_80101618, ftKb_PrSpecialNStart_Coll
text_corpus: bot report lists 5 new matches in ftKb_SpecialNNs at 100%

key_files.role: Decompiled Kirby Pikachu/Pichu, Bowser/Giga Bowser, and Link/Young Link copy-neutral-B collision/index helpers.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftKb_SpecialNPk.c added 123 deleted 5
raw_diff: ftKb_PkSpecialN_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialNStart_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialN_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialNEnd_Coll, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB394
text_corpus: bot report lists 5 new matches in ftKb_SpecialNPk at 100%

key_files.role: Decompiled Kirby Sheik-copy neutral-B end animations for ground and air.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftKb_SpecialNZd.c added 44 deleted 2
raw_diff: ftKb_SkSpecialNEnd_Anim and ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim
text_corpus: bot report lists 2 new matches in ftKb_SpecialNZd at 100%

key_files.role: Decompiled Ice Climbers up-B/down-B related functions and added a local forward declaration for ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftPp_SpecialS.c added 116 deleted 5
raw_diff: ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Phys, ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C, ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Enter
text_corpus: bot report lists 5 new matches in ftPp_SpecialS at 100%

key_files.role: Recovered three ftIceClimberAttributes float fields at x88, x8C, and x90 from padding.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftPopo/types.h added 3 deleted 1
raw_diff: ftIceClimberAttributes replaces u8 _88[0x94 - 0x88] with float x88, x8C, x90

key_files.role: Updated Kirby header prototypes for newly referenced/decompiled functions.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftKb_Init.h added 2 deleted 1
raw_diff: fn_80100E0C prototype replaces decomp placeholder; fn_80105978 prototype added

key_files.role: Added Nana init prototype used by Popo up-B throw animation logic.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftNn_Init.h added 1 deleted 0
raw_diff: bool ftNn_Init_8012309C(Fighter_GObj* gobj) added and used by ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_0_Anim

what_changed: PR body says it decompiled 5 functions in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c for Kirby copying Ness neutral-B, 2 in ftKb_SpecialNZd.c for Kirby copying Sheik neutral-B, 5 in ftKb_SpecialNPk.c for Kirby copying Pikachu/Bowser neutral-B, and 5 in ftPp_SpecialS.c for Ice Climbers up-B/down-B.
Bot report lists 17 new matched functions totaling +2676 matched bytes and an overall matched-code increase from 57.58% to 57.65%.
Kirby Ness-copy code added a small static inline helper, ftKb_SetNsFlashAttr, to reset several specialn_ns motion variables and ns_flash_gobj before both ground and air end animations.
Several Kirby copy-special collision functions transition from ground to air motion states while preserving current animation frame and choosing motion IDs by copied fighter kind or facing direction.
Ice Climbers code added Popo/Nana coordination checks through Player_GetEntityAtIndex, ftNn_Init_8012309C, part joint lookup, and state transitions for up-B throw behavior.
Popo attributes padding at offsets x88, x8C, and x90 was replaced with float fields used by the newly decompiled air up-B entry/physics code.
Headers were updated with prototypes for functions that became callable from newly decompiled source.

smart_moves.text: Factored repeated Kirby Ness-copy attribute reset logic into a static inline helper instead of duplicating the field assignments in both ground and air end animations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SetNsFlashAttr added and called by ftKb_NsSpecialNEnd_Anim and ftKb_NsSpecialAirNEnd_Anim

smart_moves.text: Recovered Popo attribute offsets as typed float fields and used them directly in decompiled movement calculations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPopo/types.h adds x88, x8C, x90; ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter divides velocities by da->x84 and da->x88; ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Phys calls ftCommon_Fall(fp, da->x8C, da->x90)

smart_moves.text: Used switch statements for copied-fighter motion selection where the decompiled behavior branches by hat kind or motion ID group.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_PkSpecialN_Coll switch on fv.kb.hat.kind; ftKb_KpSpecialN* switch on GKOOPS/KOOPA; ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB394 nested switches for LINK/CLINK motion IDs

smart_moves.text: Updated owning headers when newly decompiled functions were referenced across files.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_Init.h adds fn_80100E0C and fn_80105978 prototypes; ftNn_Init.h adds ftNn_Init_8012309C prototype

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a decompiled fighter function exposes nearby attribute offsets as real numeric fields, promote the padding to typed fields in the character attributes struct and use those fields at call sites.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPopo/types.h replaces padding with float x88, x8C, x90
raw_diff: ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter and ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Phys use the recovered float fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: For copied Kirby specials, motion-state selection can depend on the copied character kind, while collision transitions preserve the current animation frame and use specific transition flag constants.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_PkSpecialN_Coll selects Pichu/Pikachu air neutral-B motion states
raw_diff: ftKb_KpSpecialNStart_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialN_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialNEnd_Coll select Giga Bowser/Bowser air motion states and preserve fp->cur_anim_frame

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small static inline helpers are useful when multiple matched functions share the same source-level field reset sequence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SetNsFlashAttr consolidates repeated specialn_ns assignments and is called from two matched end-animation functions
text_corpus: bot report shows the affected functions reached 100%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Cross-character partner logic in Ice Climbers functions may require checking the partner entity by player slot, validating partner state, and reading partner part joints before updating Popo state vectors.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Phys reads Nana entity at player index 1 and part FtPart_L4thNb joint
raw_diff: ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_0_Anim checks ftNn_Init_8012309C before calling ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C

naming_conventions.convention: Use short local names fp, fp2, da, co, msid, gobj, and nn_gobj for fighter pointer, data attributes, co attributes, motion ID, fighter object, and Nana partner object roles.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: new functions consistently use Fighter* fp, ftKb_DatAttrs* da, ftCo_DatAttrs* co, FtMotionId msid, Fighter_GObj* nn_gobj

naming_conventions.convention: Keep address-style or unknown semantic names when meaning remains unresolved, such as fn_80105978, ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C, and ftKb_SpecialNPr_80101560.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: prototypes and functions retain address-style names for several newly decompiled or referenced functions

naming_conventions.convention: Attribute fields with unknown meaning may remain offset-style names, such as x88, x8C, x90, specialn_pr_unk1, and specialn_pr_unk2, rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPopo/types.h adds x88, x8C, x90; ftKb_SpecialNPr_80101560/80101618 use specialn_pr_unk1 and specialn_pr_unk2

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used narrow PAD_STACK locals in several newly decompiled functions to satisfy stack layout for matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(4 * 8) in ftKb_SkSpecialNEnd_Anim and ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim
raw_diff: PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(40), and PAD_STACK(8) in ftPp_SpecialS.c functions
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: PR body and bot report provide match results, but no inline explanation says why each PAD_STACK was required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Preserved repeated GET_FIGHTER reloads and scoped blocks in some functions, likely to match register or lifetime behavior.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter reloads fp via GET_FIGHTER more than once
raw_diff: ftKb_PkSpecialN_Coll and ftKb_KpSpecialN* use inner scoped blocks around msid and anim_frame
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The diff demonstrates the shape and the bot report says the functions matched, but the PR does not explicitly document the register-lifetime rationale.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used switch/case grouping for frame-counter trigger points and motion-ID classification.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SkSpecialNEnd_Anim and ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim group cases 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, 17
raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB394 groups Link and Young Link neutral-B motion IDs
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Supported by matched diff shape; not accompanied by author explanation.

searchable_terms: ft: decompile Kirby & Ice Climbers special moves
follow_up_queries: ftKb_SpecialNPk hat.kind FTKIND_PICHU FTKIND_GKOOPS switch motion

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR #2289 title, author, state, URL, dates, changed files
human_pr_text: summary and verification claims
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for all 7 changed files
changed_files.jsonl: added/deleted/hunk counts
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report with 17 new matches and 100% per function
notes: No review comments were present in the loaded slice, so review_feedback is empty.
Verification is supported by PR body and bot report, but no local build logs beyond the loaded bot report and author text are included.
Several matching tactics such as PAD_STACK and repeated reloads are visible in the diff, but the PR does not explain their necessity; they are recorded as tactics with caveats, not general standards.
The PR body says 5+2+5+5 functions, while the bot report details 17 new matches; these are consistent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover typed attribute fields when matched code proves float offsets
accepted_candidate_records.text: In fighter decomp, if newly matched code uses adjacent character-attribute offsets as floats, replace padding with typed fields and use those fields directly instead of pointer arithmetic or padding access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPopo/types.h adds float x88, x8C, x90
raw_diff: ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter uses da->x88; ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Phys uses da->x8C and da->x90

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep unresolved fighter helper names address-style
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR left several decompiled or newly referenced functions with address-style names where semantic meaning was not established, while still adding truthful prototypes where needed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80100E0C, fn_80105978, ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C, ftKb_SpecialNPr_80101560 names retained

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK may be needed for specific matched fighter functions but should remain narrow
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR used small PAD_STACK calls inside individual matched functions and reported 100% objdiff matches; the evidence supports this as a narrow tactic in this PR, not as broad style guidance.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK uses in ftKb_SpecialNZd.c and ftPp_SpecialS.c
text_corpus: bot report lists affected functions at 100%

source_update_candidates.title: Conservative naming example for unresolved fighter functions
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding PR #2289 as an example where address-style function names and offset-style attribute names were retained while decompiling matched code, avoiding speculative semantics.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: address-style names retained in headers and function definitions
raw_diff: ftPopo/types.h adds x88, x8C, x90 rather than guessed semantic names
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concrete example aligned with existing conservative-naming guidance, but curator review should decide whether it is useful enough to add.

source_update_candidates.title: Narrow PAD_STACK examples from matched fighter functions
source_update_candidates.text: Consider using PR #2289 as an example that PAD_STACK can appear in matched fighter functions only with narrow scope and verifier evidence, and should not be generalized without explanation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK in ftKb_SkSpecialNEnd_Anim, ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter, ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C, ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Enter
human_pr_text: all functions 100% match via objdiff
text_corpus: bot report lists 17 new matches at 100%
source_update_candidates.reason: Evidence supports use in this PR, but the author did not document why each padding site was needed; source update should preserve that caveat.

source_update_candidates.title: Kirby copy-special files touched by PR #2289
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2289 decompiled Kirby copy-special functions in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, ftKb_SpecialNPk.c, and ftKb_SpecialNZd.c, covering Ness, Pikachu/Pichu, Bowser/Giga Bowser, Link/Young Link, Jigglypuff/Purin, and Sheik-copy neutral-B related code visible in the diff.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Kirby Ness, Sheik, Pikachu/Bowser neutral-B summary
raw_diff: changed Kirby special files and function bodies
source_update_candidates.reason: May help source-path lookup, but should be curated as a path fact rather than inferred globally.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the visible repeated GET_FIGHTER reloads, inner scopes, or declaration ordering as a general style rule; the PR lacks author explanation and only shows they were part of matched output.
Do not infer semantics for x88, x8C, x90, specialn_pr_unk1, specialn_pr_unk2, fn_80105978, or ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C beyond the names and uses shown in the diff.
Do not claim reviewer intent or requested changes; the loaded slice contains no review comments.
Do not treat the Claude Code footer as evidence of correctness or original-source style.
